project: Nucleus reverse componentization pack for Blender-to-Roblox production
interpretation: The organelle is the animal eukaryotic nucleus from the current project. This pack starts with the final organelle and reverse-breaks it into individually designable Blender pieces.
style: stylized-realistic, colorful, biologically accurate, Roblox-friendly, animation-ready

groups.name: WholeCell_Context_Integration
groups.goal: Make the nucleus feel assembled into a single animal eukaryotic cell, with cytoplasm, ER side, and socketed placement.
groups.review_question: Does the nucleus clearly belong inside one cohesive cell rather than floating as an isolated prop?

groups.name: RootRig_Pivots_LOD
groups.goal: Create master pivot, axes, LOD switches, cutaway controller, and global parent relationships.
groups.review_question: Can the organelle be repositioned, rotated, scaled, and switched between review modes without breaking alignment?

groups.name: NuclearEnvelope_DoubleMembrane
groups.goal: Build the animation-ready outer membrane, inner membrane, perinuclear lumen, seams, and cutaway rims as separate pieces.
groups.review_question: Can a learner immediately see that the envelope has two membranes with a space between them?

groups.name: NuclearPoreComplex_System
groups.goal: Build NPCs as selective protein machines made of rings, lobes, gate cores, filaments, and baskets.
groups.review_question: Do the pores look like regulated complexes rather than open holes?

groups.name: NuclearLamina_SupportMesh
groups.goal: Build a gold lamin lattice under the inner membrane with detachable mitosis fragments and chromatin anchor nodes.
groups.review_question: Does the lamina clearly support the inner membrane and provide anchor points?

groups.name: Nucleoplasm_InternalMedium
groups.goal: Build the inner semi-fluid volume, particle field, and flow curves without obscuring chromatin.
groups.review_question: Does the interior feel like a transparent functional volume rather than empty space?

groups.name: GenomeSystem_Chromatin_Chromosomes
groups.goal: Build interphase chromatin, active transcription regions, heterochromatin, and hidden condensed chromosomes for mitosis.
groups.review_question: Can animations show chromatin activity and condensation without implying DNA leaves the nucleus?

groups.name: Nucleolus_Subregions
groups.goal: Build the nucleolus as a non-membrane body with subregions and rRNA/ribosome biogenesis markers.
groups.review_question: Does the nucleolus look like a dense region, not a separate membrane-bound organelle?

groups.name: NucleocytoplasmicTransport_CargoPaths
groups.goal: Build animated import/export cargo, path curves, arrows, and gate-linked transport cues.
groups.review_question: Can import and export be animated in opposite directions through specific NPCs?

groups.name: RoughER_ContinuityConnection
groups.goal: Build the rough ER connection to the outer nuclear membrane and preserve the ER lumen/perinuclear continuity cue.
groups.review_question: Is the outer nuclear membrane visibly continuous with rough ER?

groups.name: MitosisBreakdown_ReassemblyParts
groups.goal: Create envelope fragments, detached NPC pieces, lamina fragments, and reassembly target sockets.
groups.review_question: Can envelope breakdown and reassembly be understood as a biological sequence?

groups.name: Hotspots_Labels_ReviewUI
groups.goal: Create Roblox interaction hotspots, optional labels, review icons, and animation triggers.
groups.review_question: Can a player or reviewer inspect and trigger the functional systems clearly?

groups.name: Cameras_Lights_Checkpoints
groups.goal: Create cameras, lighting, labels, review checkpoints, and pass/fail controls.
groups.review_question: Can the model be reviewed from consistent high-quality 3D angles?

MAT_CellMembrane_ClearBlue.texture: transparent glass-like membrane, soft rim highlight, very faint procedural noise

MAT_Cytoplasm_BlueGlass.texture: transparent volume with faint suspended specks and soft volumetric feel

MAT_OuterMembrane_Purple.texture: satin membrane, procedural fine bump, subtle color variation, rounded bevels

MAT_InnerMembrane_DeepPurple.texture: darker satin membrane, smoother than outer membrane, light bump

MAT_PerinuclearLumen_AmberGlow.texture: transparent amber additive glow to reveal the space between membranes

MAT_NPC_Protein_Violet.texture: smooth rounded protein lobes with bevels and slight roughness

MAT_NPC_Gate_LilacGlass.texture: translucent gel-like selective gate, soft emission pulse

MAT_Lamina_Gold.texture: warm gold tube lattice, clean bevels, low roughness

MAT_Nucleoplasm_BlueTransparent.texture: glass-like internal sphere with light fog, low visual opacity

MAT_Chromatin_Purple.texture: beveled DNA/chromatin curves, slight emission, varied thickness

MAT_Chromatin_CyanHighlight.texture: secondary highlighted chromatin strands for active regions

MAT_Chromosome_Magenta.texture: smooth condensed chromosome surface with broad rounded grooves

MAT_Nucleolus_DeepViolet.texture: dense bumpy spheroid with procedural noise displacement

MAT_Nucleolus_MagentaSpeckles.texture: small surface speckles and mottled subregion patches

MAT_Cargo_Protein_Green.texture: glossy green cargo spheres with small NLS tag bead

MAT_Cargo_RNA_Pink.texture: pink squiggle curves with emissive direction dots

MAT_Cargo_Ribosome_Magenta.texture: paired rounded subunit particles, glossy, slightly different sizes

MAT_RoughER_BluePurple.texture: folded ER sheet membrane, satin, ribosome dots on cytoplasmic surface

MAT_Ribosome_Orange.texture: tiny orange dots with simple glossy material

MAT_MembraneFragment_Purple.texture: broken curved membrane pieces with bevels

MAT_Hotspot_GoldEmission.texture: gold icon plaques with mild emission and pulsing rim

generation_order: G00 Whole-cell context and nucleus socket
G01 Root rig, pivots, controllers, LODs
G02 Outer/inner membrane panels, perinuclear lumen, cutaway rims
G03 NPC master module, then NPC instances
G04 Lamina support mesh and anchors
G05 Nucleoplasm transparent volume and particles
G06 Chromatin, transcription hotspots, condensed chromosomes
G07 Nucleolus subregions and rRNA/ribosomal markers
G08 Transport cargo, paths, and gate pulse controller
G09 Rough ER connection and ER lumen continuity
G10 Mitosis breakdown/reassembly fragments and target sockets
G12 Cameras, lighting, review checkpoints, validation passes

animation_plan.name: A01_Interphase_Idle
animation_plan.pass: Subtle motion only; nucleus remains intact.

animation_plan.name: A02_Protein_Import_NLS
animation_plan.pass: Green cargo moves cytoplasm to nucleus through NPC.

animation_plan.name: A03_RNA_Export
animation_plan.pass: RNA/ribosomal cargo exits; chromatin stays inside.

animation_plan.name: A04_Transcription_Hotspot
animation_plan.pass: RNA appears from chromatin hotspot without moving DNA.

animation_plan.name: A05_Mitosis_Breakdown
animation_plan.pass: Chromatin condenses, nucleolus fades, lamina fragments, envelope panels split, NPCs detach.

animation_plan.name: A06_Nuclear_Reassembly
animation_plan.pass: Envelope reforms, pores reinsert, lamina returns, nucleolus and chromatin return to interphase state.

checkpoints.name: Scene setup and naming
checkpoints.pass_criteria: All collections and object names follow manifest exactly. No stray nucleus objects outside hierarchy.

checkpoints.name: Whole organelle cohesion
checkpoints.pass_criteria: Nucleus sits in cytoplasm with clear socket and ER contact side; whole cell context reads in 3D.

checkpoints.name: Double nuclear envelope
checkpoints.pass_criteria: Outer membrane, inner membrane, perinuclear lumen, and cutaway rims are all visible.

checkpoints.name: Envelope panelization
checkpoints.pass_criteria: Outer/inner/lumen segments are separate and can split for mitosis without gaps/overlap errors.

checkpoints.name: NPC biological readability
checkpoints.pass_criteria: NPCs are protein complexes with rings, lobes, gates, filaments/baskets on hero pores.

checkpoints.name: Lamina support
checkpoints.pass_criteria: Gold lamina lies beneath inner membrane and anchors peripheral chromatin.

checkpoints.name: Internal volume
checkpoints.pass_criteria: Nucleoplasm is transparent, textured, and does not obscure chromatin/nucleolus.

checkpoints.name: Genome system
checkpoints.pass_criteria: Interphase chromatin and condensed chromosomes are separate states; DNA never exits the nucleus.

checkpoints.name: Nucleolus
checkpoints.pass_criteria: Nucleolus is non-membrane, bumpy, subregioned, and able to fade/reform in mitosis.

checkpoints.name: Transport
checkpoints.pass_criteria: Protein import and RNA/ribosomal export use NPC paths in opposite directions.

checkpoints.name: ER continuity
checkpoints.pass_criteria: Outer membrane visibly connects to rough ER and perinuclear lumen continuity is shown.

checkpoints.name: Mitosis animation
checkpoints.pass_criteria: Breakdown and reassembly can be understood using panels, fragments, chromosomes, and target sockets.

checkpoints.name: Roblox export readiness
checkpoints.pass_criteria: Meshes are separate, origins sensible, surfaces thick enough, triangle budget recorded, materials portable.

checkpoints.name: Final 3D quality
checkpoints.pass_criteria: Model has high-quality depth, texture, shadows, bevels, and readable stylized realism.

components.geometry_strategy: Master empty and optional transparent ellipsoid guide for animal cell boundary, scaled about X=20 Y=14 Z=9.

components.biological_function: Defines whole-cell context so the nucleus is one organelle inside a larger animal cell.
components.animation_function: Parent all whole-cell context assets; optional cell idle sway.

components.pivot_origin: Origin at world center.

components.pass_criteria: Cell boundary is visible but does not obscure the nucleus.

components.geometry_strategy: Semi-transparent ovoid volume inside the cell shell, with small low-poly suspended specks and soft motion curves.
